---
pagetitle: "Ran Wang | Academic Website"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.site-hero role="img" aria-label="Blue mountain ranges fading into the distance"}
::: {.hero-inner}
::: {.hero-copy}
# Ran Wang

::: {.hero-kicker}
Urban & Regional Economics · Scientific Mobility · Regional Innovation
:::

::: {.hero-summary}
I am an empirical researcher trained in economic geography. I study scientific mobility, institutional platforms, and regional innovation using longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities. My work combines discrete-choice models, network analysis, and spatial data methods.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[View Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-hero-primary}
[Download CV](assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf){.btn .btn-hero-secondary}
[Selected Projects](projects.qmd){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
[GitHub](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
:::
:::

::: {.photo-credit}
Photo: John Rodenn Castillo / Unsplash
:::
:::
:::

::: {.page-shell}
## Research Focus

::: {.section-intro}
My research examines how scientific careers, institutional platforms, and city-level opportunity structures shape the geography of knowledge production. I focus on careful data construction, transparent definitions of mobility and affiliation, and empirical designs that connect individual trajectories with urban and regional innovation systems.
:::

## Research Themes

::: {.theme-grid}
::: {.theme-card}
<div class="eyebrow">Mobility</div>

### Scientific Mobility and Destination Choice

I study how scientists move across institutions and cities, with attention to the information and opportunity structures visible before a move. Current work uses event-specific destination-choice data to compare observed moves with plausible alternative destinations.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
<div class="eyebrow">Regions</div>

### Regional Innovation and Urban Networks

This work examines how cities and regions accumulate research capacity through institutions, collaboration ties, and mobility flows. The emphasis is on measured associations between network position, urban opportunity, and innovation-related activity.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
<div class="eyebrow">Methods</div>

### Relational Data and Network Methods

I build scientist-institution-city datasets that make affiliations, publications, collaboration ties, and mobility events analytically distinct. This supports network analysis, spatial comparison, and reproducible workflows for auditing source coverage.
:::
:::

## Selected Work

::: {.work-grid}
::: {.work-card}
<div class="status-badge">Accepted Article</div>

### Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective

A related Chinese article studies the spatial structure and determinants of top global biomedical scientists' mobility from an urban network perspective. Final publication metadata will be added after confirmation.
:::

::: {.work-card}
<div class="status-badge">Working Paper</div>

### Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists

This working paper examines how pre-move publication-location exposure is associated with observed destination choice among candidate cities. Restricted source data and full manuscript materials are not publicly released here.
:::

::: {.work-card}
<div class="status-badge">Public Repository</div>

### Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit

A public repository demonstrates synthetic author-institution-city linkage, geocoding QA, validation checks, duplicate diagnostics, and reproducible documentation without redistributing restricted bibliographic data.

[View repository](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research/author-institution-city-linkage){.text-link}
:::
:::

## Quick Links

::: {.quick-links}
[Research](research.qmd)
[Projects](projects.qmd)
[CV](cv.qmd)
[Contact](contact.qmd)
:::
:::
